seoTitle: Fix Instagram Established Presence Branded Content Error
metaDescription: Instagram's Established Presence check blocks branded content tools for new or low-activity accounts. Learn how to audit your status and request a review.
intro: If Instagram is flagging your account as ineligible for branded content because of an 'established presence' problem, it usually means the platform hasn't yet verified enough authentic activity on your profile. This guide explains what triggers the flag - new accounts, low followers, or inconsistent posting - and how to work toward eligibility. You'll also see how to request a manual review once you've addressed the underlying issues.

steps.heading: Check your Branded Content eligibility status
steps.body: Open your professional dashboard and review your current branded content eligibility to confirm the established presence flag is what's blocking you.

steps.heading: Switch to a Professional Creator or Business account
steps.body: If you haven't already, moving to a Professional account type is often required before Instagram will consider your profile for branded content tools.

steps.heading: Build up authentic activity and engagement
steps.body: Increase your posting frequency and engagement in a way that signals genuine, ongoing activity rather than a one-off push, since Instagram is checking for a consistent history.

steps.heading: Clear any hidden violation flags
steps.body: Review your account for content or behavior that might be tripping Instagram's automated review, and address anything that could be read as a policy violation.

steps.heading: Request a final review from Instagram
steps.body: Once your account reflects a more established presence, submit a manual review request from the professional dashboard to have the ineligible status reassessed.

faq.question: How long does it take to fix an established presence problem on Instagram?
faq.answer: It depends on your account's history - Instagram is looking for a consistent pattern of authentic activity, so it can take anywhere from a few weeks to longer if your account is very new.

faq.question: Does follower count affect branded content eligibility?
faq.answer: A low follower count combined with inconsistent posting is one of the common reasons Instagram flags accounts as not having an established presence.

faq.question: Do I need a Business or Creator account to use branded content tools?
faq.answer: Yes, switching to a Professional Creator or Business account is generally a prerequisite for accessing Instagram's branded content and partner monetization tools.

faq.question: Can I appeal if I think Instagram made a mistake?
faq.answer: Yes, once you've reviewed your account status you can request a manual review from Instagram to have the ineligible status reconsidered.
